executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 103 organizations compromised in a single 48-hour period. The Qilin ransomware group leads the assault with 21 victims including Eastern Townships School Board and Workers Health & Safety Centre. Canada Goose has been hit twice by different groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters), and critical infrastructure including Peterborough Public Health, Markham Stouffville Hospital, Municipality of North Perth, and Eeyou Communications Network are confirmed breached. The Clop gang alone has claimed 14 Canadian victims spanning law firms, technology providers, and manufacturing.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 21 Canadian victims including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra law firm, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, and Berts Electric
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - Double extortion ransomware operations with victim shaming sites

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is under siege by ransomware groups with 103 confirmed victims across all sectors in 48 hours. Critical infrastructure has been severely impacted including healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Bison Family Medical, Thunder Bay Counselling), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart, Collège Supérieur De Montréal), public sector (Municipality of North Perth, Peterborough Public Health, Workers Health & Safety Centre), and telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network). No CCCS advisories were issued despite this mass compromise event.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose breached by two separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters). Active Green + Ross automotive retailer and Kids & Company childcare services also compromised.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database leak via Everest ransomware. Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi. Multiple accounting firms targeted including Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, and SMT Lawyers.

report_2w.executive_summary: Adobe Acrobat/Reader zero-day CVE-2026-34621 has been actively exploited since December 2025 and requires immediate patching across all systems. CISA has added six actively exploited vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, including flaws in Fortinet, Microsoft, and Adobe software. High-profile breaches continue: Booking.com confirmed a customer data breach, ShinyHunters claims a major breach of Rockstar Games (GTA 6 developer) with ransom deadline today, and Amtrak allegedly lost 9.4 million Salesforce records. A critical CVSS 10.0 supply chain vulnerability in the Axios JavaScript library (CVE-2026-33634) has been patched after enabling full cloud compromise via prototype pollution.

items.description: Adobe Acrobat/Reader zero-day actively exploited since December 2025
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

canada_landscape.summary: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-007 on April 7, 2026 regarding CVE-2026-35616 impacting Fortinet FortiClientEMS, requiring immediate attention by Canadian organizations. No direct cyberattacks on Canadian critical infrastructure were reported in this period. Political news dominates Canadian feeds with Prime Minister Mark Carney's Liberal Party securing majority government in special elections.
canada_landscape.retailers: CBC investigation found Loblaw and Sobeys overcharging customers for underweight meat products (not a cybersecurity incident, but operational/compliance issue). No cybersecurity incidents identified for Canadian Tire, SportChek, or Shoppers Drug Mart.
